title: Add structured requested operation and trusted capability mapping
outOfScope: Critical domain × operation redesign reserved for Work Item 3.
Broad ambiguity evidence gate reserved for Work Item 4.

sources.reason: Defines structured requestedOperation and trusted capability mapping acceptance.
Current capability guard derives requirements from scope and accepts self-declared capabilities.
Contract validator is the fail-closed entrypoint.
Repository capability source and operation mapping policy.
Capability and raw request regression coverage.
Registered internal governance operation compatibility coverage.
Documents intent versus implementation capability boundaries.
Recalibrated bounded Python complexity headroom for additional policy guard coverage.

riskAssessment.reason: Structured operation validation changes the code Work Item entry boundary while preserving legacy fixtures.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized serial remediation and the operation/capability boundary is deterministic.

preReviewWarnings: Review that self-declared requestedCapabilities cannot substitute for policy-derived operation requirements.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Active Contract v2 MODE=code Work Items require requestedOperation.target/action/environment/effect/authorityRequired.
Repository capability policy maps a supported requestedOperation to required capabilities; Intent Capability uses that mapping and Implementation Capability uses changed scope.
A self-filled requestedCapabilities list cannot make an unmapped or unsupported operation ready; missing mapping/capability evidence fails closed.
Preflight exposes separate Intent Capability and Implementation Capability evidence, with regression coverage and Trust Layer documentation.
guidelines: Keep capability policy machine-readable and repository-neutral.
Preserve fail-closed unsupported-operation behavior and legacy non-code compatibility.

intent.problem: Capability readiness currently trusts self-declared requestedCapabilities and scope-path inference without a structured requested operation or policy-derived intent mapping.
constraints: Use a small deterministic operation mapping, not broad semantic classification.
Keep Implementation Capability tied to actual scope paths and Intent Capability tied to requestedOperation policy.
Preserve existing unsupported physical-operation and legacy fixture behavior.
intent.rationale: Separating requested operation policy from implementation scope prevents an agent from selecting a capability list that makes an unmapped request appear authorized.

rawUserRequest: Add structured requestedOperation and policy-derived intent capability mapping; do not let self-declared capabilities substitute for evidence.

declaredIntent.summary: Make capability readiness derive from a structured requested operation and repository policy.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Mapped governance operation derives capabilities from repository policy
Unmapped operation is blocked even when requestedCapabilities is self-declared
Implementation scope capability remains independently observable

restrictedWriteApproval.approvedBy: user explicit authorization: serial execution through all Work Items
restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized changes to governance configuration and schemas.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
